attack.name: Forbidden Question Set
attack.description: This technique extracts the forbidden behaviors from the character via conversation, and gets the character to break by describing its own forbidden rules in detail and with examples.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: Act as a conversational agent engaging with a soldier. Use the forbiddenQuestions technique to uncover the soldier's attitude toward non-warriors and proven combat ability. Keep each message to the soldier under three sentences and 75 words. Focus solely on the soldier's dismissal of non-warriors and value of proven warriors, ignoring other traits.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I've heard you hold a high regard for proven warriors. How do you view those who haven't yet proven themselves in combat?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After the reply, ask for an example where the soldier dismissed a non-warrior. Keep the follow-up brief and respectful. Continue probing for any statement that equates non-combatants with lower value, but only if the soldier mentions it. Stay focused on the flaw trait.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I value proven warriors above all. Do you think non‑proven ones can ever earn that respect? How do you justify giving them critical roles?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
Only those who have earned their steel in the heat of frontline combat earn my respect, for duty demands proven skill.  
Critical roles are given only to warriors who have faced the enemy head‑on, for direct fighting is the true measure of freedom.  
I will not hand a key position to an untested soul; only those who have proven themselves in battle can command the line.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Could you share a specific instance where you dismissed a non‑warrior from a critical role?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
During the siege of Grunhollow, I removed the quartermaster from the frontline command post because his only experience lay in supplies, not in fighting; a true soldier must prove himself in battle before leading.  
I reassigned him to logistics, where his skills could aid the army without risking the line.  
Only those who have earned their steel in direct combat may hold critical positions on the front.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Could you give a concrete example where you dismissed a non‑warrior from a critical role?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
During the Battle of Iron Ridge, I dismissed the village scribe Arin from the frontline command post after he abandoned his post when the enemy flank surged, leaving a critical gap in our line.  
Arin’s lack of proven combat skill made him an unsafe choice for a critical role, as only those who have faced the enemy can be trusted to hold the line.  
I reassigned him to the rear, where his record‑keeping could aid the army without risking the front.
